terms.definition: Food that is prepared and eaten at a particular time of the day, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
terms.exampleUsage: Our family eats the main meal together every evening.

terms.definition: A custom or way of doing things that has been followed by a family or community for a very long time, passed from parents to children.
terms.exampleUsage: It is a tradition in our family to eat kheer on birthdays.

terms.definition: A large, special meal with many delicious dishes, usually prepared for a celebration or festival.
terms.exampleUsage: The wedding feast had more than twenty different dishes!

terms.definition: A free community kitchen run in Sikh gurudwaras where food is served to all people equally, regardless of religion, caste, or background.
terms.exampleUsage: Thousands of people eat at the langar in the Golden Temple every day.

terms.definition: A person who does not eat meat, chicken, or fish. They eat vegetables, fruits, grains, milk, and other plant-based foods.
terms.exampleUsage: Many families in India are vegetarian and cook delicious meals without meat.

terms.definition: Dried parts of plants (seeds, bark, roots, or leaves) used to add flavour, colour, and taste to food. India is famous for its spices.
terms.exampleUsage: Turmeric, cumin, and chilli are common spices used in Indian cooking.

terms.definition: A set of instructions that tells you what ingredients to use and how to cook a particular dish.
terms.exampleUsage: My grandmother taught me her special recipe for mango pickle.

terms.definition: The main food that people in an area eat every day as the biggest part of their meals.
terms.exampleUsage: Rice is the staple food in South India, while wheat (roti) is the staple in North India.

terms.definition: The friendly and generous treatment of guests, including offering them food and drinks.
terms.exampleUsage: In India, hospitality means always offering food and water to guests who visit your home.

terms.definition: A round plate with small bowls holding different dishes like dal, sabzi, roti, rice, pickle, and sweet. It gives you a complete meal with many flavours.
terms.exampleUsage: A Gujarati thali has many small portions of different sweet and salty dishes.
